factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields rising sharply as inflation fears spike; Bloomberg reports 'Treasuries Lead Global Bond Yields Higher on Inflation Angst' with oil-driven inflation keeping rates elevated for longer. Higher real yields directly suppress gold demand.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY climbing above 99.00 for fifth consecutive day on strong retail sales data (+0.5% m/m) and Fed shift expectations. Stronger USD makes gold more expensive for foreign buyers and is single biggest headwind for XAUUSD.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent Surprises)
factors.summary: Japan PPI surged 4.9% y/y (expected +3.0%), well above consensus; energy shock from Iran war driving import prices +17.5% y/y. War-driven inflation reduces Fed rate-cut odds, pushing real yields higher and pressuring gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy Guidance
factors.summary: Fed Governor Barr stated 'I haven't decided on what to do at June FOMC meeting' but acknowledged energy inflation shock; NY Fed President Williams sees no case for rate moves and policy in 'good place.' Messaging leans hawkish given inflation, reducing safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: WTI near $97.50, oil +1%+ on Trump-China deal and Trump's Iran warnings; Strait of Hormuz effectively closed. Elevated energy inflation is bearish for real yields (bullish gold normally), BUT it's also reducing rate-cut odds (bearish gold). Mixed net effect.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Summit & Trade Optimism
factors.summary: US-China trade truce extended, China committing double-digit billions in ag/oil purchases, 200 Boeing jets ordered. Risk-on sentiment reduces safe-haven demand for gold; equity futures initially rallied (then retreated on Iran rhetoric).

factors.name: Equity Market Sentiment
factors.summary: Global stocks initially surged (Korea KOSPI hit 8,046) then reversed 2%+ on Trump's Iran impatience warnings. Chop but net risk-off at close; gold not benefiting as safe-haven given DXY strength dominates.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh news on PBoC/RBI/Turkey gold purchases in this window. Structural bid is constant but not a tactical driver over 24-72h horizon.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations (Breakevens)
factors.summary: 5y5y forward inflation expectations rising due to energy shock; Michigan near-term inflation expectations elevated. But longer-term expectations (Fed Williams) described as 'stable.' Rate market is pricing less-dovish Fed, negative for gold.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: XAUUSD down -0.37% in 4h and -1.93% in 24h. Move already underway in SELL direction; not extended yet but momentum is down and building.

screening.narrative: Gold is under structural pressure from a perfect storm of DXY strength (99.10+, five-day rally), rising real yields (energy-shock inflation pushing Treasuries higher despite no near-term Fed move), and reduced safe-haven demand (Trump-Xi trade optimism, equity recover attempts). Japan PPI surprise (4.9% vs 3.0% expected) is fueling global yield spike. Price action shows a -1.93% 24h selloff already in motion—this is Phase 2 (move building, not extended yet). The only offsetting factor is oil/geopolitical risk (Iran tensions, Hormuz closure), but that's being priced as inflation headwind (bearish for real yields, bearish for gold) rather than flight-to-safety.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI/Core CPI release (date TBD in next 2 weeks) — hot print would accelerate yields higher, bearish gold
FOMC minutes or Powell speech — any hawkish signal would reinforce DXY strength
BoJ rate decision (June likely) — any hike would steepen USD/JPY uptrend, bearish gold via yen carry unwind
Fed Barr/Williams further remarks — watching for pivot on inflation vs. employment trade-off
Trump-Iran escalation risk — could flip to flight-to-safety bid if headlines worsen
China PBOC any policy shift — yuan weakness (6.8415 fix today) suggests continued PBOC caution, supports USD strength
screening.risk_warning: Immediate invalidation: (1) Sharp geopolitical escalation or military action in Middle East (Hormuz closure, Iran-Israel direct conflict) would trigger safe-haven bid, overriding DXY strength. (2) Unexpected dovish Fed pivot (Barr/Powell dove signals) would spike real yields downward, bullish gold. (3) US equity crash would trigger flight-to-safety, gold spikes regardless of DXY. (4) A surprise cool CPI print in next 2 weeks would crater yields and reverse this SELL bias. Watch 24h price action — if XAUUSD bounces >0.8% off these levels, reassess Phase 2 pullback scenario.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (down -1.93% 24h, momentum building). Do NOT chase short here; wait for retest of $4,650–$4,680 resistance (intraday highs) for a cleaner short entry, or wait for pullback failure at support ($4,600) before adding. If geopolitical headlines flip, exit immediately. Confidence capped at 6 because major catalyst (FOMC, CPI) could flip the bias sharply higher in 1-2 weeks.
